nuggets.description: Augusto discusses his three-year struggle with commitiment to his partner, Lydia. He realizes he has used her child as a 'deflector' for his fear of responsibility and has a pattern of 'avoiding deeper waters' until things become toxic.

Andre reframes the story of Genesis, suggesting that Adam's sin was not just listening to Eve, but failing to take responsibility for her. This is linked to the difference between a boy (who consumes) and a man (who provides containment and protection).

nuggets.topic: Fear vs. Faith: A Practical Compass
nuggets.description: Andre defines fear as 'faith that the world is bad' and that things won't work out. He challenges Augusto to see that his inability to make a decision is a submission to fear, and that serving God requires living outside of that spirit.

action_items.task: Schedule follow-up call with Augusto for Saturday (free).
Ruminate on the 'Fear vs. Faith' definition and the archetype of responsibility.
